questions.objective_ref: Choose an evaluation metric by matching it to the cost of errors in your domain.
Choose an evaluation metric by matching it to the cost of errors in your domain (accuracy can lie on imbalanced classes).
Decide how many labeled test examples you need so the score is trustworthy, not noise.
Decide a trustworthy test set: examples must be held-out (and representative).
Set a pass/fail threshold in advance so the result cannot move the goalposts.
Set a pass/fail threshold tied to the stakes of the task.
Assemble the three decisions (metric, test-set size, threshold) into a one-page evaluation plan.
